steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence ends a noun phrase (‘newts’) and then continues with ‘for instance’ followed by an independent clause. The blank must correctly punctuate the parenthetical transitional phrase ‘for instance’ within the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘newts: for’: a colon after a noun phrase would introduce a list or explanation, but ‘for instance’ is not a list item — it is a parenthetical transition. A colon here is grammatically incorrect. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘newts. For’: a period would leave ‘In a JSON file storing data about fire belly newts’ as a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘newts; for’: a semicolon joins two independent clauses, but what precedes the blank is not an independent clause, so a semicolon cannot be used here. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘newts, for’: parenthetical transitional phrases like ‘for instance’ are set off with commas. A comma after ‘newts’ correctly introduces the parenthetical before the independent clause continues. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
